title: --net=none does not create loopback device
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

getaddrinfo() fails to find ipv4 or ipv6 support, and result in error.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

Compile and run the following program:

```
#include <assert.h>
#include <sys/types.h>
#include <sys/socket.h>
#include <netdb.h>
#include <string.h>

int main(int argc, char *argv[])
{
    struct addrinfo ai, *res = NULL;

    memset(&ai, 0, sizeof(ai));
    ai.ai_flags = AI_CANONNAME | AI_ADDRCONFIG;
    ai.ai_family = PF_INET; /* PF_UNSPEC works */
    ai.ai_socktype = SOCK_STREAM;

    assert(getaddrinfo("127.0.0.1", NULL, &ai, &res) == 0);
}
```


**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:            1.4.5-dev
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.12.6
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

  go version: go1.12.6
  podman version: 1.4.5-dev
host:

      runc version 1.0.0-rc8+dev
      commit: e3b4c1108f7d1bf0d09ab612ea09927d9b59b4e3
      spec: 1.0.1-dev
  SwapFree: 7651110912
  SwapTotal: 8187277312

  kernel: 5.1.12-300.fc30.x86_64
```

comments.body: This looks rootless. Can you give additional details on your setup? How are you invoking Podman?

Hmm. It seems like you're expecting to find a loopback in the container
when there is none. That means we might need to use CNI to configure even
net=none containers which is messy...

On Thu, Jul 11, 2019, 03:02 Marc-Andre Lureau <notifications@github.com>
wrote:

> podman run -it --security-opt seccomp=unconfined --net=none fedora
>
> —
> You are receiving this because you commented.
> Reply to this email directly, view it on GitHub
> <https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/3535?email_source=notifications&email_token=AB3AOCFBCUBUPJHVH5QNJDTP63LJNA5CNFSM4H7HPZB2YY3PNVWWK3TUL52HS4DFVREXG43VMVBW63LNMVXHJKTDN5WW2ZLOORPWSZGODZVXAHY#issuecomment-510357535>,
> or mute the thread
> <https://github.com/notifications/unsubscribe-auth/AB3AOCHBY564EAWIJR7QJBDP63LJNANCNFSM4H7HPZBQ>
> .
>

running ip addr, the loopback is there, with ipv4/ipv6.

But libc has some trouble with the config apparently. Are you able to reproduce?

Seems like a rather large difference between Docker and Podman
```
# podman run --net=none -v /usr/sbin/ifconfig:/usr/sbin/ifconfig fedora ifconfig
```
Versus
```
# docker run --net=none -v /usr/sbin/ifconfig:/usr/sbin/ifconfig fedora ifconfig
lo: flags=73<UP,LOOPBACK,RUNNING>  mtu 65536
        inet 127.0.0.1  netmask 255.0.0.0
        loop  txqueuelen 1000  (Local Loopback)
        RX packets 0  bytes 0 (0.0 B)
        RX errors 0  dropped 0  overruns 0  frame 0
        TX packets 0  bytes 0 (0.0 B)
        TX errors 0  dropped 0 overruns 0  carrier 0  collisions 0
```
Can't we just use `/etc/cni/net.d/200-loopback.conf`  for this?  (Knowing almost nothing about CNI.)

I think we can - but right now we aren't running CNI at all for `--net=none` containers.

I also don't know if we can tell CNI to *only* join one network - right now it always joins the default network.
@mheon Any new ideas on this?  @giuseppe Do you think you could look into this?
I can take this as part of the v6 work
This issue had no activity for 30 days. In the absence of activity or the "do-not-close" label, the issue will be automatically closed within 7 days.
@mheon I think this is still important.  Have you been able to make any progress on this?
